concerns.text: Task T022 (CSA Index construction) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on the output of T015 (Data Cleaning/Merging) to access the merged dataset. The task description implies reading the merged data to construct the index, creating a hidden dependency on US1 completion. It cannot run in parallel with T012-T015.

Task T023 (Model fitting) is marked [P] but depends on the output of T022 (CSA Index construction) and T024 (VIF diagnostics) to define predictors and check collinearity. The model cannot be implemented or tested without the feature engineering and diagnostics tasks completing first.

Task T031 (Scatter plot generation) is marked [P] but depends on the output of T023 (Model fitting) to visualize the relationship between CSA Index and Food Security. The visualization task cannot proceed without the model results.

Task T035 (Leave-one-region-out) is marked [P] but depends on the output of T023 (Model fitting) to perform cross-validation on the fitted model. It cannot run in parallel with the model implementation.

Task T019 (Main entry point for data pipeline) is placed after T018 but before the US1 checkpoint. While logically an orchestration task, it depends on T012-T018 being implemented. Marking it as a standalone task without explicit dependency on the completion of T012-T018 in the ordering logic is risky; it should be grouped as the final step of US1 implementation, not a parallel task.

The 'Parallel Opportunities' section claims 'Models within a story marked [P] can run in parallel'. However, T022 (Index), T023 (Model), T024 (Diagnostics), and T025 (Correction) form a strict linear chain (Index -> Diagnostics -> Model -> Correction). The [P] tags on these tasks are semantically incorrect and misleading for the execution order.
